qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsynchronized global agenda cache lets concurrent requests crash the Go process
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a concurrency-driven denial of service in the agenda cache path at /campaign/:campaignId/agenda. The advisory is an authorization bypass on /api/admin/* caused by missing Gin middleware abort logic, so the vulnerable code and root cause do not align.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Client-only registration window enforcement permits out-of-window tutor and tutee registration changes
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a business-rule enforcement gap on campaign registration endpoints, where the backend omits registration status and date checks. The advisory instead concerns non-admin access to /api/admin/* continuing past a forbidden response because the Gin admin middleware fails to abort.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is specific to /api/admin/* authorization bypass caused by the Gin admin-auth middleware failing to abort request handling after detecting a non-admin user, while the two finalized findings target a registration-window validation gap and an agenda-cache race.
evidence: The advisory summary and remediation describe standard-user access to /api/admin/* endpoints continuing after a FORBIDDEN condition because the fix adds a missing c.Abort() call in Gin authentication middleware (commit 15ae474).

registration_window_bypass concerns POST /campaign/:campaignId/tutor/registrations and POST /campaign/:campaignId/tutee/registrations, where registrationStatus and registration date checks are missing; it does not involve /api/admin/* or the admin middleware chain.

agenda_cache_concurrent_map_crash concerns GET /campaign/:campaignId/agenda reaching unsynchronized global maps in GetMonthAgenda and causing a concurrent-map fatal; it is an availability race, not an authorization bypass.
